title: AI answer without sources vs source grounding display vs citation display vs confidence / uncertainty display vs streaming response vs correction feedback
summary: Flag AI answer without sources when a generated answer makes evidence-dependent claims with no visible source route or no-source state. Use the related patterns for answer-wide evidence coverage, claim-level citations, reliability display, source-pending generation, or post-answer correction.
decisionRules: Choose AI answer without sources when a generated answer includes factual, policy, legal, medical, financial, operational, customer-impact, or data-derived claims and the UI provides no citation, grounding, source scope, not-searched label, retrieval failure, or verification path.

Choose source grounding display when the product can show answer-wide source scope, searched sources, retrieved sources, used evidence, unsupported claims, partial support, permission-limited sources, stale sources, and retrieval failures.
Choose citation display when the design problem is mapping specific claims to inline citation markers, selected source previews, source excerpts, and open-source actions.
Choose confidence / uncertainty display when users need calibrated model reliability, uncertainty reason, review threshold, and action gating; confidence does not prove sources exist.
Choose streaming response when answer text and source events are still arriving, and keep source status pending until retrieval and final text settle.
Choose correction feedback when users need to report a wrong, unsupported, stale, missing-source, or wrong-source answer after it appears.
A no-source answer must say whether sources were not searched, no supporting source was found, retrieval failed, permission blocked the source, or the answer is only a draft from model knowledge.
Do not present related links, a Learn more list, decorative citation numbers, or a high-confidence badge as evidence used by the answer.
Gate copy, publish, apply, send, export, or automation when source-dependent claims remain unsourced, or preserve the no-source status in the resulting artifact.
If a user intentionally continues without sources, record that decision and keep the answer labelled unsourced rather than upgrading it to verified.
failureModes: A fluent policy answer is sent to a customer with no citation, grounding, or not-searched disclosure.
Retrieval fails but the UI hides the failure and returns a confident model-memory answer.
A high confidence badge is shown where users needed source evidence.
Related links appear under the answer and are mistaken for used evidence.
Copy final strips the no-source warning from the answer.
Permission-limited sources vanish, making the answer look fully sourced.

sources.note: Supports visible source references for web-search-backed output.
Supports annotations and included search results for file-backed output.
Supports retrieval over selected source stores rather than source-free answers.
Supports source scope and authenticated knowledge access.
Supports communicating AI uncertainty and helping users understand system capabilities and limitations.
Supports announcing changed source, retrieval, and verification status without moving focus.
